residence.note: Practiced law in Brattleboro after Londonderry.

parents.father_research_notes: Father James Martin of Landgrove VT. Not built.

narrative_blocks.header: Law clerkship at fourteen; federal bench at sixty
narrative_blocks.body: James paid his own tuition at age fourteen by working in a chair factory, producing maple sugar, and clearing land. He read law with Hoyt Henry Wheeler — and forty years later succeeded that same Wheeler on the federal bench when Theodore Roosevelt appointed him United States District Judge for Vermont in 1906.

narrative_blocks.header: Speaker of Vermont House for four straight years
narrative_blocks.body: James served in the Vermont House of Representatives 1874-1882 and was elected Speaker 1878-1882 — four consecutive years presiding over the chamber. Subsequent service: State Tax Commissioner three terms, US Attorney for Vermont 1898-1906, and Federal District Judge from 1906 to his death in 1915.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost three children of first marriage by 1882
narrative_blocks.body: James's first marriage in 1869 to Delia Howard ended with her death in 1881 and the loss of all three of their children — Jimmie who lived 1873 to 1880, an unnamed infant who lived only days in 1877, and a second Delia who lived from 1881 to 1882. None survived to adulthood, and James remarried in 1884 to Jessie Dewey — with whom he had three daughters who all reached adulthood.

education.school_name: Read law with Hoyt Henry Wheeler

career.notes: Purchased Charles N. Davenport's Brattleboro practice.
Succeeded John H. Senter; succeeded by Alexander Dunnett.

career.organization: United States District Court for the District of Vermont
career.notes: Recess appointment by Theodore Roosevelt October 20, 1906; formally nominated December 3, 1906; Senate confirmed December 11, 1906. Filled position vacated by Hoyt Henry Wheeler — Martin's former law-reading mentor. Served until his death.

institutions.institution_blurb: Director of the National Life Insurance Company alongside his Dewey in-laws.

burial.plot_notes: Morningside Cemetery, Brattleboro VT — alongside James.
Morningside Cemetery, Brattleboro VT — alongside parents.

narrative_blocks.body: Margaret served as Vermont's female member of the Republican National Committee 1932-1936. She was a delegate to the 1928 Republican National Convention and alternate delegate in 1932 — carrying her father Judge James Loren Martin's Republican legacy into the next generation of Vermont state politics.

burialCemetery.name: Morningside Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Morningside Cemetery, Brattleboro Vermont. Burial site of US District Judge James Loren Martin (X02377, Wikipedia notable Vermont Speaker), his second wife Jessie Dewey Martin (TD0151), and their daughter Margaret Susan Martin Tucker (TD0156, Vermont Republican National Committee member 1932-1936). Three generations of Vermont Republican political family.

INST112.name: National Life Insurance Company
INST112.notes: Founded 1850 by Dr. Julius Yemans Dewey (X02373) at Montpelier Vermont. One of the ten oldest insurance companies in the United States. Julius personally delivered the company's first claim remittance. Multi-generation Dewey family enterprise: Julius founder/president to 1877; son Charles Dewey (TD0139) succeeded as president; son Edward Dewey (TD0140) was assistant secretary 1865-1877 then vice-president 1877-1897; son-in-law James Loren Martin (X02377, US District Judge for Vermont) served as director. Still active 21st century with 250,000+ policyholders, 3,500 brokers in all 50 states; one of Montpelier's largest private employers.

INST058.name: Dartmouth College
INST058.notes:
John Henry Colby (X00729) class of 1885; John Noyes Colby (HD2304) class of 1916. Colby family three-generation Dartmouth line (John Freeman Colby 1859, John Henry 1885, John Noyes 1916).
